intro: Created in 1934, the New York City Housing Authority (NYCHA) is the largest public housing authority in North America. It provides affordable housing to more than 511,000 authorized residents — about 1 in 16 New Yorkers — across 177,565 apartments in 2,410 buildings and 335 conventional public housing and PACT developments spanning all five boroughs.

cardHighlights.function: Operates and manages the city's public housing developments
cardHighlights.scale: Houses more than 511,000 residents in 177,565 apartments
